title: Phenol-Chloroform Extraction and Ethanol Precipitation of RNA
author: Tomasz Suchan
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.rzvd766

steps.title: Adjust the reaction volume to 180...
steps.instruction: Adjust the reaction volume to 180 μl by adding 160 μl nuclease-free water. Add 20 μl of 3 M sodium acetate, pH 5.2 or 20 μl of 5 M ammonium acetate (1/10 of the total volume), mix thoroughly.

steps.title: Extract with an equal volume (200...
steps.instruction: Extract with an equal volume (200 μl) of 1:1 phenol/chloroform mixture. Vortex and centrufugate at maximum speed for 5 min.

steps.title: Precipitate the RNA by adding 2...
steps.instruction: Precipitate the RNA by adding 2 volumes of -20°C 100% ethanol. Incubate at –20°C or on dry ice for at least 30 minutes.

steps.title: Remove the supernatant and rinse the...
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant and rinse the pellet with 500 μl of cold 70% ethanol.

steps.title: Resuspend the RNA in 50 μl of 0
steps.instruction: Resuspend the RNA in 50 μl of 0.1 mM EDTA. Store the RNA at –20°C or below.

steps.title: Collect the aqueous (upper) phase and...
steps.instruction: Collect the aqueous (upper) phase and transfer to a new tube.

steps.title: Collect the pellet by centrifugation at 4°C for 30 min
steps.instruction: Collect the pellet by centrifugation at 4°C for 30 min.

steps.title: Quick spin and collect the last drop of ethanol
steps.instruction: Quick spin and collect the last drop of ethanol.
